title: Component Styles Sidecar
description: Consumer-side CSS declarations for a single component. Top-level keys are selectors (anatomy parts like "root"/"track", state shorthands like "hover"/"checked", variant modifiers like "--md", or compound selectors like ":has(.switch__input:checked) .switch__track"). Each value is a property-keyed map; the property name is the entry's identity. Entry values are structured resolutions: `resolvesTo` (slot or global token graph), or `literal` with required `platforms` array.

additionalProperties.description: A selector's block: keys are CSS property names; values are styleEntry resolutions.

styleEntry.description: Resolution for a single CSS declaration. Either `resolvesTo` (slot reference or global token graph) or `literal` (with required `platforms`).

oneOf.description: Slot or global-token reference. First segment of the dotted path decides namespace: <cssPrefix>.* resolves to a slot declared in this contract's tokens.json; core.* / semantic.* resolves to the global token graph.

resolvesTo.description: Dotted path. Component-local slot (e.g. "switch.color.track.background.default") or global token (e.g. "semantic.color.background.tertiary").

fallback.description: Optional fallback for the var() reference at the consumer site. Usually unneeded — the slot's own fallback chains via the cascade.

literal.description: Hardcoded CSS value emitted verbatim when the build target matches `platforms`.

platforms.description: Targets that should honor this declaration. Required on `literal`; optional on `resolvesTo` (defaults to all three platforms).
